targets.rawSql: SELECT s.recorded_at AS time, s.latency_ms AS value, e.name || ' — ' || CASE WHEN e.probe_type = 'icmp' THEN 'ICMP reached' WHEN s.status_code >= 500 THEN 'Server error (' || s.status_code || ')' WHEN s.status_code >= 400 THEN 'HTTP response (' || s.status_code || ')' ELSE 'Reached (' || COALESCE(s.status_code::text, 'unknown') || ')' END AS metric FROM ping_samples s JOIN endpoints e ON e.id = s.endpoint_id WHERE $__timeFilter(s.recorded_at) AND s.latency_ms IS NOT NULL ORDER BY 1, e.sort_order, e.name

SELECT s.recorded_at AS time, -50.0 AS value, e.name || ' — Down (not reached)' AS metric FROM ping_samples s JOIN endpoints e ON e.id = s.endpoint_id WHERE $__timeFilter(s.recorded_at) AND s.error IS NOT NULL ORDER BY 1, e.sort_order, e.name

SELECT DISTINCT ON (e.id) e.name AS endpoint, s.latency_ms AS latency_ms, s.recorded_at AS time FROM endpoints e JOIN ping_samples s ON s.endpoint_id = e.id WHERE s.latency_ms IS NOT NULL ORDER BY e.id, s.recorded_at DESC

SELECT s.recorded_at AS time, e.name AS endpoint, s.error, s.status_code FROM ping_samples s JOIN endpoints e ON e.id=s.endpoint_id WHERE $__timeFilter(s.recorded_at) AND (s.error IS NOT NULL OR s.status_code >= 400) ORDER BY s.recorded_at DESC LIMIT 100

SELECT e.name AS endpoint, e.probe_type AS probe_type, CASE WHEN s.id IS NULL THEN 'No data' WHEN s.error IS NOT NULL THEN 'Down' WHEN s.status_code >= 500 THEN 'Server error' WHEN s.status_code >= 400 THEN 'HTTP response' ELSE 'Healthy' END AS health, s.latency_ms, s.status_code, s.recorded_at AS last_ping, s.error FROM endpoints e LEFT JOIN LATERAL (SELECT * FROM ping_samples WHERE endpoint_id=e.id ORDER BY recorded_at DESC LIMIT 1) s ON TRUE ORDER BY e.sort_order, e.name
